sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि।
यच्छ्रेय एतयोरैकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम्॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
sannyāsaṃ karmaṇāṃ kṛṣṇa punar yogaṃ ca śaṃsasi |
yac chreya etayor ekaṃ tan me brūhi suniścitam ||
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: O Krishna, You praise renunciation of actions, and again You praise Yoga (performing action). Which one of these two is better? Tell me that for certain.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences cognitive dissonance after receiving seemingly contradictory instructions—first to act (Ch.2-4) and now to renounce action—mirroring the anxious paralysis of modern decision-fatigue when two equally endorsed life-paths appear mutually exclusive.
psychological_state: Decision-paralysis under authority conflict; seeks external certainty to escape internal ambivalence.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional torn between a high-paying job offer and a minimalist sabbatical, both endorsed by respected mentors, now asking a life-coach: 'Just tell me which one is definitely right for me.'
anchor_text: Choose action guided by wisdom, not restless craving for outcomes.
